text:
There once was a little frog named Gerald. Gerald lived near a pond. Every morning he would hop down there to see his friend Wormy. Wormy liked to talk about everything. One day Wormy said something strange. Gerald listened and tilted his nose.

"Wormy," Gerald asked carefully. "What if you could swim?"

"W-wow!" Wormy giggled. "Yes! Think about it!"

Gerald thought about swimming. He didn't quite understand how. Wormy jumped right into the water. Gerald watched. Wormy paddled! Soon, Gerald tried it too.

For days, Gerald swam with Wormy. Swimming taught him friendship. Swimming taught him patience. Swimming taught him how far they could all go.

One sunny day, Gerald realized something important.

"Wormy," he said carefully, full of gratitude. "Thanks for swimming with me."

Wormy smiled. "Anytime!"

They swam until it was time for Gerald's momma to call.
